dense_caption.overall_summary: A man in a white hazmat suit holds up a small, wooden object with a 'M' logo on it, looking shocked. The scene cuts to two other men, one in a green hoodie and one in a blue turtleneck, who look on with serious expressions. The man in the hazmat suit then reveals a large, homemade machine with a glass dome, labeled 'TIME MACHINE,' and explains that it works as a time-freezing device.; speech: Man in hazmat suit: Whaa.. what happened?! ...it works as a... Time Freezing Machine.; acoustics: 1) Tone of speech: The inventor's tone is excited and slightly manic. 2) Background sounds or music: A low, tense, electronic hum begins as the time machine is revealed.; TIME MACHINE,; attributes: camera_state: The scene starts with a close-up on the wooden object, then cuts to a medium shot of the two men. It then cuts to a medium shot of the man in the hazmat suit, focusing on the time machine., video_background: The setting appears to be a dimly lit room or office with dark walls. A few pictures are visible on the wall behind the man in the hazmat suit., storyline: The scene introduces a mysterious time machine and its inventor, who is explaining its function to two other characters. The inventor's excitement contrasts with the other two's skepticism., shooting_style: The scene uses intercutting between the inventor and the two onlookers to show their different reactions to the time machine.

transcript_spans.text: What the? Like I said, it works. It's a time freezing machine. Freezing? What is your plan? We're going to rob a bank. In broad daylight. A big high street. Split three ways. Pack up the gears. It's time. I don't trust you. Maybe you never froze the time. You had an ice cream before. Just a sleight of hand. Alright. Not this one.
Just turn off the antenna and press this button. Alright buddy, we're gonna rob. You stay here, we split two ways. Safety always. I want this.
